summary: Acoustic gems on Mountains of the Moon and fiery Lovelight
blurb: The set is highlighted by a soaring acoustic jam on "Mountains Of The Moon" and Pigpen’s powerhouse rendition of "Turn On Your Lovelight." Jerry’s fluid solos keep the energy high, while Phil anchors the group with solid bass. A short, festival‑style performance that captures the band’s 1968 raw excitement.
review: December 20, 1968 at Shrine Exposition Hall delivered a concise but electrifying three‑song set that feels like a snapshot of the Dead in their early psychedelic era. The night opened with the relentless drive of "The Eleven," then moved into an unexpected acoustic centerpiece – “Mountains Of The Moon” – where Jerry’s guitar weaves delicate textures and Phil lays down a sturdy, melodic bass line. Pigpen stole the show on “Turn On Your Lovelight,” his harmonica wailing over a rolling rhythm section that kept the crowd moving. Although both existing soundboard tapes exhibit a noticeable hiss, the Miller capture (gd1968-12-20.sbd.miller.89663.sbeok.flac16) offers the cleanest listen and brings out the nuance of the acoustic jam. For Deadheads hunting an early‑Dead gem that showcases the band’s improvisational spirit and Pigpen’s raw charisma, this performance is a must‑hear.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Both recordings praise the musical performance – standout acoustic jam on "Mountains Of The Moon" and a fiery "Turn On Your Lovelight" – with no consistent negative comments about playing. Mixed sentiment only concerns tape hiss, not the show itself, so a moderate positive adjustment is applied.

best_recording.reason: Soundboard source with the least hiss and clearer instrument separation, highlighting the acoustic jam and Pigpen’s performance.

key_highlights: Acoustic guitar jam on "Mountains Of The Moon" showcases Jerry’s nuanced playing.
"Turn On Your Lovelight" delivers a high‑energy Pigpen showcase.
Tight rhythm section from Mickey, Billy, and Phil keeps the set driving forward despite recording hiss.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers fluid, SG‑style solos and a standout acoustic guitar jam on "Mountains Of The Moon".
band_performance.Phil: Provides solid, melodic bass work throughout, especially praised on the acoustic centerpiece.
